title: Getting Through the Holidays as a Caregiver
summary: The holidays add a layer of pressure on top of ongoing caregiving — a few adjustments make the season more manageable.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Getting Through the Holidays as a Caregiver | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: The holidays add a layer of pressure on top of ongoing caregiving — a few adjustments make the season more manageable.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Scaling back tradition is allowed, and often welcomed by the whole family.
Accepting help with hosting or cooking reduces real burden.
Planning rest time around the busiest days matters as much as planning the events themselves.

paragraphs: Many caregivers feel pressure to preserve every holiday tradition exactly as it's always been, but scaling back — a smaller gathering, a simpler meal, skipping one event — is a completely reasonable adjustment, and often a relief to everyone involved, not just the caregiver.

Actively accepting offers of help — someone else hosting, bringing a dish, taking on decorations — reduces real workload, and deliberately planning rest time around the most demanding days helps prevent the season from becoming pure depletion.

nextSteps: Decide which traditions matter most this year and let go of the rest.
Say yes to specific offers of help rather than declining out of habit.
Block out rest time around the busiest days, not just after them.
disclaimer: General guidance — every family's traditions and capacity look different this time of year.
